sn35.30:1.1: "Bhikkhus, I will teach you the way that is appropriate for uprooting all conceivings.sn.iv.22Listen to that and attend closely, I will speak … .
sn35.30:1.2: "And what, bhikkhus, is the way that is appropriate for uprooting all conceivings?Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu does not conceive the eye, does not conceive in the eye, does not conceive from the eye, does not conceive, ‘The eye is mine.’He does not conceive forms … eye-consciousness … eye-contact … and as to whatever feeling arises with eye-contact as condition—whether pleasant or painful or neither-painful-nor-pleasant—he does not conceive that, does not conceive in that, does not conceive from that, does not conceive, ‘That is mine.’
sn35.30:1.3: "He does not conceive the ear … He does not conceive the mind … mental phenomena … mind-consciousness … mind-contact …sn.iv.23and as to whatever feeling arises with mind-contact as condition … he does not conceive that, does not conceive in that, does not conceive from that, does not conceive, ‘That is mine.’
sn35.30:1.4: "He does not conceive all, does not conceive in all, does not conceive from all, does not conceive, ‘All is mine.’
sn35.30:1.5: "Since he does not conceive anything thus, he does not cling to anything in the world. Not clinging, he is not agitated. Being unagitated, he personally attains Nibbāna. He understands: ‘Destroyed is birth, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is no more for this state of being.’
sn35.30:1.6: "This, bhikkhus, is the way that is appropriate for uprooting all conceivings."
